<!--
Thanks for contributing! Keep the summary focused on the WHY.
See CONTRIBUTING.md for build/test and commit conventions.
-->

## Summary

<!-- What does this change and why? -->

## Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Refactor / cleanup
- [ ] Test / CI

## Testing

- [ ] `make test` passes (unit tests — array is mocked)
- [ ] Added/updated unit tests for new logic

**Hardware testing:**

- [ ] Not hardware-tested (logic/spec only)
- [ ] Tested on a real array — model & microcode: `__________`, Proxmox VE: `__________`

<!-- If you changed an array-facing assumption, quote the API behaviour you observed. -->

## Checklist

- [ ] Docs updated in the same change (the relevant `docs/*` and, if behaviour changed, `docs/INTEGRATION_CHECKLIST.md`)
- [ ] `CHANGELOG.md` and `debian/changelog` updated for user-visible changes
- [ ] Conventional-commit subject (`feat:`/`fix:`/`docs:`…), under 72 chars
- [ ] Commits are signed off (`Signed-off-by:`) per the DCO
- [ ] No credentials/secrets in code, tests, or examples
